2605.25717.title: FLOATBench: A Dataset and Benchmark for Floating Offshore Wind Turbine Tower Fatigue
2605.25717.paper_summary: Most of the world's offshore wind resource lies in waters too deep for fixed-bottom foundations, making floating offshore wind turbines (FOWTs) essential for deep-water deployment. As the industry scales toward $22$ MW class designs, tower fatigue becomes increasingly critical because larger structures amplify the coupled aero-hydro-servo-elastic loads induced by continuous wind and wave excitation. Accurate fatigue-damage prediction is therefore central to certification, design optimization, and cost reduction. Yet the field lacks a shared surrogate benchmark: studies report different simulations, splits, and metrics, making methods difficult to compare. We present FLOATBench, a public tabular benchmark with $582{,}120$ per-section fatigue-damage labels across three $22$ MW FOWT tower geometries, derived from $19{,}404$ high-fidelity OpenFAST simulations across the three towers ($6{,}468$ per tower: $1{,}078$ aligned wind/wave operating points $\times$ six turbulence seeds), labeled at $30$ cross-sections per tower. FLOATBench includes a regime-aware alpha-shape partition of the joint wind/wave operating envelope, stratifying test points into in-train, interpolation, and extrapolation regimes. It is paired with a reproducible evaluation harness covering three protocol levels: random validation (E1), within-tower regime-aware evaluation (E2), and cross-tower transfer (E3). The regime-aware protocol reveals rank shifts between global and extrapolation performance that random-split leaderboards cannot detect. To the authors' knowledge, FLOATBench is the first FOWT fatigue benchmark for tabular surrogate modeling, and offers an evaluation protocol that generalizes to engineering surrogates defined over physical operating envelopes. Dataset and code available at: https://github.com/Joao97ribeiro/FLOATBench.
2605.25717.paper_summary_zh: 世界上大部分的離岸風能資源位於水深過大的地方，無法使用固定底座基礎，因此浮動離岸風力渦輪機（FOWTs）對於深水部署至關重要。隨著行業向 $22$ MW 級設計擴展，塔架疲勞變得越來越關鍵，因為較大的結構會放大由持續的風和波浪激勵所引起的耦合氣動-水動-伺服-彈性負荷。因此，準確的疲勞損傷預測對於認證、設計優化和成本降低至關重要。然而，該領域缺乏共享的替代基準：研究報告中使用了不同的模擬、分割和指標，使得方法難以比較。我們提出了 FLOATBench，一個公共表格基準，擁有 $582{,}120$ 個每部分的疲勞損傷標籤，涵蓋三種 $22$ MW FOWT 塔幾何形狀，這些標籤來自於 $19{,}404$ 次高保真度的 OpenFAST 模擬，涵蓋三座塔（每座塔 $6{,}468$ 次：$1{,}078$ 個對齊的風/波操作點 $\times$ 六個湍流種子），在每座塔的 $30$ 個橫截面上標記。FLOATBench 包括一個考慮運行狀態的 alpha-shape 分割，將測試點分為訓練內、插值和外推區域。它與一個可重複的評估工具相配合，涵蓋三個協議層級：隨機驗證（E1）、塔內考慮運行狀態的評估（E2）和跨塔轉移（E3）。考慮運行狀態的協議揭示了全球和外推性能之間的排名變化，而隨機分割的排行榜無法檢測到。據作者所知，FLOATBench 是首個針對表格替代建模的 FOWT 疲勞基準，並提供了一個可推廣至定義於物理操作包絡的工程替代品的評估協議。數據集和代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/Joao97ribeiro/FLOATBench。
2605.25717.author: João Alves Ribeiro et.al.
2605.25717.authors: João Alves Ribeiro, Bruno Alves Ribeiro, Francisco Pimenta, Sérgio M. O. Tavares, Faez Ahmed
